summary: Phase 3 Claims frontend wiring tested via Playwright as admin & patient. Sidebar 'Claims' nav, dashboard 'Claims queue' button, /billing/claims queue (42 rows, status filter Needs fixes -> 4 matched rows), claim detail (validation/header/diagnoses/lines sections), Run scrubber (errors panel populated), edit-diagnoses dialog, edit-lines dialog, role gating (patient pending re-verify) all worked. Three issues observed: (1) Header edit dialog failed to open after Run scrubber, (2) Submit on a 'ready' claim did not visibly transition pill to 'Submitted' within 4s, (3) Generate-claim from a self-pay invoice did not navigate (expected) but no toast keyword captured (probably auto-dismissed before assertion).

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking claim-edit-header-btn after Run scrubber on a validation_failed claim does NOT open claim-header-dialog (ch-save not rendered). Diagnoses & Lines edit dialogs DO open under same conditions, so root cause is dialog-specific (likely first useState(() => ({...claim})) initializer never re-runs and a stale claim reference may confuse mount, or button is intercepted by a re-rendered validation panel). Reproduce: open a validation_failed claim, click Run scrubber, wait for errors, click Edit on Header card.

On a 'ready' claim, claim-submit-btn click did not surface 'Submitted' pill in claim-detail within 4s. Needs verification whether POST /api/billing/claims/{id}/submit succeeded but UI didn't refresh, or call failed.

integration_issues.issue: On the first invoice (likely self-pay) the click did not navigate (correct per spec) but no visible 409 toast text could be asserted at +5s. Toast may auto-dismiss too quickly to assert in CI; consider keeping error toast visible >=8s or showing inline error.

action_items: Fix claim-edit-header-btn -> dialog not opening after a validate run on validation_failed claim (HIGH)
Verify claim-submit-btn on 'ready' status refreshes detail and pill becomes 'Submitted'
Optionally extend toast duration for Generate-claim 409 errors so users (and tests) can read it
critical_code_review_comments: ClaimDetail.HeaderEditDialog uses useState(() => ({...claim})) lazy init; if dialog opens before claim prop re-flows after refresh, form state could be stale. Consider always deriving from claim via key prop reset or controlled form.
ClaimsQueue summary 'Billed total' aggregates only the currently filtered rows, not all-claims total – label may mislead users when a non-'all' filter is active.
Generate-claim error path silently relies on toast timing; consider an inline alert on InvoiceDetail when 409 is returned so the eligibility reason persists.
seed_data_creation: None - existing seed used (42 claims, 4 validation_failed, 2 ready).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend Phase 3 already pytest-validated (69/69). Focus retest on: (a) opening claim-edit-header-btn dialog reliably, (b) submit -> Submitted transition on a 'ready' claim, (c) Generate-claim from a clearly insurance-bearing invoice (filter invoices by responsibility=insurance/mixed), (d) confirm patient role redirect from /billing/claims (logout flow via user-menu-trigger needed extra wait in last run).
rca of the issue: Header dialog mount failure suspected to be the lazy useState initializer not re-evaluating after refresh + a possible click-target scroll issue; diagnoses/lines dialogs open fine because their state is local arrays rather than spread of a possibly-stale claim object.
